2024 JYP ENTERTAINMENT SUSTAINABILITY REPORT 2 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x About This Report Report Overview This report is JYP Entertainment's fourth sustainability report, published to transparently disclose to stakeholders our sustainability, management direction and performance across economic, environmental and social dimensions under our "LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT" vision. Through the report development process, we conducted a double materiality assessment to identify the significance of both environmental and social impacts, as well as financial impacts. All the while gathering and incorporating stakeholder feedback to determine our reporting topics, JYP Entertainment is committed to disclosing and managing our medium- to long-term strategies, objectives, and operational performance through sustainability reports as transparently as possible.
Reporting Period This report covers our key sustainability activities and performance from January 1 to December 31, 2024, with some management materiality from the first half of 2025. For quantitative performance, we have included three years of data from 2022 through 2024 so that year-to-year trends can be better seen. This report was published in June 2025 with a one-year reporting cycle.
Reporting Scope The scope of this report encompasses ESG management activities and performance of our domestic headquarters, subsidiaries, and overseas affiliates. For data presented in the Appendix, the reporting boundary for each indicator is specified on page 116 of this report. Financial information aligns with consolidation standards under Korean International Financial Reporting Standards(K-IFRS). Where material changes have occurred, these are indicated through separate footnotes in the relevant sections. Reporting Principles and Standards This report has been prepared in accordance with the Global Reporting Initiative(GRI) Standards 2021, the global standard for sustainability reporting. Additionally, we have incorporated information following the principles, guidance, and indicators required by the Sustainability Accounting Standards Board(SASB) industry standards. Furthermore, we support the United Nations Sustainable Development Goals(UN SDGs) and report on implementation activities aligned with each goal.
Independent Assurance In order to ensure the fairness, reliability, and completeness of this report's preparation process and all information, BSI Korea, an independent assurance organization, was commissioned to conduct third-party assurance of this report. In addition, the assurance of GHG emissions and energy use was conducted by DNV Korea, an independent assurance organization. The results of the assurance of this report and the results of the assurance of GHG emissions and energy use can be found in the respective assurance opinions.
Contact Information for the Report Address: 205, Gangdong-daero, Gangdong-gu, Seoul(Seongnae-dong, JYP CENTER) Responsible Department: JYP Entertainment ESG Council E-mail: esg@jype.com Website: www.jype.com Cover Story Clear skies and nature-inspired backgrounds add a message of transparency and connection, helping to express JYP's authenticity in creating a sustainable future together.
3 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP Profile Founder/Chairman Message CEO Message About JYP Our History Leader’s Code Our Business JYP Artists Appendix ESG Data GRI Index SASB Index UN SDGs GHG Verification Statement Third-party Assurance Statement ESG Performance Environmental Environmental Management Climate Action Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value Social Human Rights Management Employees Artists Users Partners Health and Safety Social Contributions Governance BOD and Committees Shareholders Integrity and Compliance Management Information Security 4 5 6 7 8 9 11 101 117 120 121 122 124 33 36 41 49 57 63 70 75 77 82 88 91 92 96 Way to Sustainable Entertainment ESG Governance ESG Strategy ESG Highlights Stakeholder Engagement Double Materiality Assessment JYP DEI 14 15 16 17 18 22 Contents 4 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Founder/Chairman Message Founder/Chairman Message Greetings, this is J.Y. Park First of all, I would like to express my sincere gratitude to our fans and all stakeholders for your continued support and warm encouragement.
Since the foundation of the company, the slogan “LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT” has carried a meaning beyond simply aspiring to lead the industry. It reflects our firm commitment to ‘becoming a leader that drives positive change in the world’.
To achieve this, we endeavor to keep our planet, society, and people at the heart of every thought and action. Internally, we strive to build an environment where our artists, trainees, and employees can grow in both body and mind. Externally, we are committed to caring for the planet we inhabit, supporting children’s dreams, and fulfilling our social responsibilities.
This report contains the passion and efforts we have dedicated throughout 2024, as well as the direction we seek to pursue moving forward. JYP will continue its journey to create a better future by delivering positive influence in the world. We hope our sincerity reaches you, and that you will walk this path with us.
J.Y. Park Founder, JYP Entertainment 5 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x CEO Message To the stakeholders of JYP Entertainment, Greetings, This is Jung Wook, CEO of JYP Entertainment.
It is a pleasure to present JYP Entertainment’s(“JYP”) fourth Sustainability Report, highlighting our key achievements and efforts in sustainability over the past year. JYP has continued to drive innovation in the global music industry while maintaining steady growth. In 2024, we recorded KRW 601.8 billion in revenue and KRW 128.3 billion in operating profit, reflecting strong financial results. These were made possible by our commitment to innovative music content, the remarkable success of our artists, and the loyal support of fans worldwide. Stray Kids achieved their sixth consecutive No. 1 on the Billboard 200 chart, TWICE attracted 1.5 million attendees across 27 countries during their world tour, and DAY6 solidified their position as a leading K-POP band by topping music charts. NEXZ debuted successfully in May 2024, followed by KickFlip in January 2025, both earning enthusiastic responses from the global market. We will not rest on these accomplishments. Guided by our ESG vision, “Leader of Change,” we will continue to pursue key priorities for a sustainable future.
We will embed sustainability into our operations and uphold diverse values.
JYP has articulated its commitment to sustainability by stipulating in the preamble of its Articles of Incorporation that it “leads a sustainable entertainment industry, provides innovative content, and practices principled management.” This serves as a clear expression of our intent and direction toward sustainable management. We are committed to fostering shared growth with all stakeholders through responsible business practices including human rights management, mutual growth with partners, environmental protection, community engagement, and shareholder value enhancement. Furthermore, JYP is cultivating a corporate culture where every member is respected and able to grow regardless of nationality, gender, age, or cultural background. Building on this foundation, we aim to provide stakeholders with diverse and inclusive content experiences.
We are committed to preserving a sustainable environment for future generations.
JYP is taking active steps to address climate change and contribute to a healthier planet. As part of our efforts, we are pursuing greenhouse gas reduction through our ‘2050 Carbon Neutrality Roadmap’ and proudly became the first company in the domestic entertainment industry to achieve RE100. In 2024, our subsidiary BLUE GARAGE joined the RE100 initiative, and we plan to gradually expand the use of renewable energy across all of our operations. To help establish a sustainable concert culture, we are carrying out initiatives that consider both social and environmental impacts, such as enhanced safety management, improved accessibility, energy efficiency, and waste reduction. In addition, we are committed to applying eco-friendly materials and packaging and accelerating digital transformation in our album and merchandise businesses.
We will build a trusted company through a commitment to transparency.
To uphold ethical and trustworthy management, JYP continues to strengthen its governance. In 2024, we established an Information Security Team and appointed CISO and CPO to enhance information and personal data protection. We are systematically managing security risks and safeguarding our information assets by developing relevant policies and guidelines. In alignment with the enhancement of our anti-bribery management system(ISO 37001), we have established a Code of Conduct for Ethical Management, setting clear behavioral standards for all members of JYP and reinforcing transparency across the organization. Furthermore, we support a fair and safe work environment through a globally-aligned internal audit system and the JYP whistleblowing platform.
Together with all stakeholders who support JYP, we will continue our journey as a leader that drives positive change in the world. Thank you.
Wook Jeong CEO, JYP Entertainment 6 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x About JYP JYP, LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT Since its establishment in 1996, JYP Entertainment has been committed to leading the entertainment industry. Under the leadership of Asia’s top producer J.Y. Park, the company has elevated the global standing of K-POP by debuting world-renowned artists such as 2PM, DAY6, TWICE, Stray Kids, ITZY, NiziU, Xdinary Heroes, NMIXX, NEXZ, and KickFlip.
JYP has expanded its global presence by establishing JYP CHINA, JYP JAPAN, and JYP USA, thereby strengthening communication with the global music market and further extending the reach of K-POP. In the platform business, we are advancing and scaling IP-based business models through BLUE GARAGE. Additionally, JYP Partners is generating synergy by investing in promising startups with strong growth potential.
Through the discovery and development of creative and proactive talent, JYP Entertainment is realizing the vision of ‘LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT’ based on the core value system encompassing 'Leading Company’ and 'Respected Company’.

In addition, we are expanding the cultural value and influence of K-POP worldwide through our global fan base, playing a leading role in the global entertainment industry.
To this end, JYP Entertainment has faithfully implemented its own management philosophy, Leader’s Code, based on the vision of ‘LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT’. This is a specific code of conduct that all members of JYP Entertainment must adhere to, and it is a promise to all who love JYP’s music and performance.
JYP Entertainment will act based on truth, sincerity, humility, and a mindset of love according to the core values and code of conduct of Leader’s Code. JYP Entertainment will strive to be a leader in the entertainment industry and a globally respected company with creative ideas and efficient execution. Furthermore, with a strong commitment to legal compliance and ethical integrity, we will take the lead in fulfilling our social responsibility and spreading a positive influence for a better society.
L E A D E R I N E N T E R T A I N M E N T Core Value system L E A D I N G C O M P A N Y R E S P E C T E D C O M P A N Y Think Brilliant ·  We keep working on ideas that can open up new possibilities for others. ·  We believe new ideas or new perspectives can change the world. Act Efficient ·  We implement ways to work not just harder, but smarter.
·  We strive to increase productivity and make more time for a life outside of work.
·  You have nothing to fear if you have nothing to hide.
·  The more successful you get, the more powerful your voice becomes. However, your voice will fall on deaf ears if you lose your integrity in the process.
·  We pursue growth with quality, not mere speed.
· We promote a culture where hard work is valued.
Integrity Humility Love Sincerity ·  We always try to see the good in others and look for ways to improve ourselves.
· Teamwork improves when we put others before ourselves.
·  Your dream gains new momentum when it is not just for yourself but for bettering the world.
·  We actively seek and adopt ideas for a healthier body and mind.
Leader’s Code 9 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Our Business •  Develop eco-friendly merchandise and expand IP-based business •  Strengthen artist branding and enhance fan engagement JYP Entertainment develops multifaceted businesses utilizing artist IP through BLUE GARAGE and our IP Strategy Office, continuously expanding licensing areas through various partnerships to align with increasing IP utilization and market demand. Furthermore, we launched 'FANS SHOP' in April 2025, integrating our online merchandise store with a community platform to enhance artist brand value while strengthening fan touch points and creating new business models.
Merchandise and IP Licensing •  Strengthen the foundation for high-quality music content production •  Expand K-POP reach through global advancement JYP Entertainment maintains a pool of talented composers and lyricists mainly through JYP Publishing, enabling us to develop and produce high-quality music content. We have systematically established the studio facilities and physical infrastructure necessary for music production, creating an efficient and stable creative environment. Additionally, we continuously expand our global network through strategic partnerships with specialized music distribution companies worldwide, contributing to the global proliferation of K-POP content.
Music & Performance •  Differentiated Artist Development and Care System •  Support balanced growth combining skill and capability JYP Entertainment identifies talent with strong values and artistic potential from the trainee stage. Through systematic and specialized training processes, we provide comprehensive support to help them grow into artists with a balanced mindset and well-rounded character alongside their talents. This reflects JYP Entertainment's commitment to nurturing artists who can positively impact society, actively supporting the harmonious expression of each individual's personality and potential.
Artist Empowerment •  Expand the scope of artists' activities •  Proactive content risk management and customized support JYP Entertainment continuously expands domestic and global presence for our artists through diverse online and offline media activities, including broadcasting, advertising, and digital content. We conduct thorough advance reviews of potential risks throughout the entire content production process and establish customized strategies reflecting each artist's characteristics and capabilities, providing professional support to ensure more stable and successful activities.

LiSA) / Super Bowl –Japanese ver.-) iHeartMedia 2024 iHeartRadio Music Awards K-POP Album of the Year (Stray Kids - 5-STAR) SSFF & ASIA Executive Committee Short Shorts Film Festival & Asia 2024 Global Spotlight Award(Stray Kids Music Drama SKZFLIX) Billboard Billboard Music Awards Top Global K-Pop Artist(Stray Kids) 2023 Korea Economic Daily, Korea Consumer Forum Selected for the 2023 Korea First Brand Awards in the ‘Male Idol’ category(Stray Kids) Billboard 2023 Billboard Women In Music Breakthrough Artist(TWICE) TIME Selected as Next Generation Leaders(Stray Kids) Korea Creative Content Agency Korea Popular Culture and Arts Award 2023 Prime Minister’s Commendation(Stray Kids) Billboard 2023 Billboard Music Awards Top K-Pop Album(Stray Kids) 2022 Korea IR Service 2022 Korea IR Grand Awards Excellence Award JYP Entertainment recognized by U.S. TIME magazine Achieves 3rd place globally and 1st place in Korea in "The World's Most Sustainable Companies 2025" JYP Entertainment has been ranked third globally and first in Korea in "The World's Most Sustainable Companies 2025," a ranking jointly published by TIME magazine and German market research firm Statista. This assessment comprehensively evaluates corporate sustainability and growth capabilities, including revenue growth rates, financial soundness, environmental performance, and social responsibility. JYP Entertainment was the only Asian company included in the global top 10. Particularly, the company received high scores in the environmental category for its significant use of renewable energy and low greenhouse gas emissions, ranking among the top 5% of all companies in terms of carbon emissions performance. Moving forward, JYP Entertainment is committed to continue building a sustainable entertainment model based on environmental responsibility, social value creation, and ethical management practices.

N M I X X 2022.02 ~ 1 0 1 3 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x WAY TO SUSTAINABLE ENTERTAINMENT ESG Governance ESG Strategy ESG Highlights Stakeholder Engagement Double Materiality Assessment JYP DEI 14 15 16 17 18 22 1 4 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x ESG Governance ESG Governance and Roles To systematically manage overall ESG strategy and implementation, JYP Entertainment has established an organic decision-making framework centered on the ESG Committee under the Board of Directors, comprising the ESG Council and ESG Working Group. The ESG Committee deliberates and decides on company-wide ESG strategies and major agenda items, while the ESG Council gathers company-wide ESG-related opinions and reviews ESG issues and initiatives. The ESG Working Group is responsible for developing specific implementation plans and executing initiatives, as well as collaborating with each department to drive practical activities. ESG activities are structured into Environmental, Social, and Governance areas, with designated departments responsible for each area. The Environmental area involves Management Support Team, IP Strategy Office, BLUE GARAGE, F&B Team, and CSR Team, focusing on greenhouse gas and energy management, waste reduction, and eco-friendly technology applications. The Social area is managed by HR Team, Safety Management Team, Performance Business Office, and CSR Team, covering human rights, talent development, occupational health and safety, and social contribution. The Governance area is overseen by IR Investment Team, Audit Office, Legal Team, and Information Security Team, responsible for the Board of Directors, integrity management, compliance management, risk management, and information security. Through this structure linking the ESG Committee to operational teams, all processes—from strategy to execution and evaluation—are systematically connected.
Environmental Social Governance · Climate Action · Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value · Energy Management · Resource Circulation ·  Implementing Eco-Friendly Business Initiatives · Human Rights Management · health and safety · Supply Chain Management · Talent Development · Employee Benefits and Welfare · Social Contributions · Compliance Management · Integrity Management · Board of Directors · Risk Management · Information Security ESG Council ESG Working Group Board of Directors ESG Committee 1 5 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x ESG Strategy and Framework Based on our core value of “LEADER IN ENTERTAINMENT," JYP Entertainment has established our ESG vision of "Leader of Change" and is building a strategic framework to realize this vision. "Leader of Change" extends beyond mere declaration to concrete action toward a sustainable future, centered on three strategic pillars. First, "Change for the Dreamers" focuses on creating an environment where everyone can realize their dreams, expanding opportunities for value creation, and establishing a fair foundation for growth. The second pillar, "Change for the Planet," represents our strategy to protect the environment and strengthen sustainability, with climate action and the promotion of eco-friendly values as key initiatives. Finally, "Change for the Society" seeks to create a fair content production environment based on collaboration with diverse stakeholders and pursue multifaceted support measures for shared growth with partners. Furthermore, as the foundation for strategy execution, we have established a governance system led by the Board of Directors and ESG Committee, strengthening our execution capabilities through stakeholder communication, ethical management, and transparent decision-making structures.

Incorporated commitment to sustainable management in corporate charter and enhanced shareholder value through stronger dividend transparency and board composition.
Implemented human rights risk mitigation activities and improvement initiatives including operating autonomous human rights organization 'URI JYP' to diversify grievance handling, conduct organizational culture assessments, and improve employee work environments.
Average monthly utilization rate increased to 63% after introducing in-house reusable cup rental system, reducing the use of 41,770 single-use items and lowering carbon emissions.
Strengthened information security governance and advanced systems through establishment of Information Security Team and appointment of CISO(Chief Information Security Officer) and CPO(Chief Privacy Officer).
Enhanced employee satisfaction through expanded welfare programs including child education support, comprehensive health examination support, and increased childbirth allowances.
Enhanced emissions data management scope by building additional Scope 3 indirect emissions inventory(Category 2, 3, and 7).
Had a positive influence through continuous charitable activities, including appointments to major donor group of various non-profit organizations, goodwill ambassador roles, and social contribution activities.

Stakeholders Communication Channels Representative Activities Key Activities 1 8 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Materiality Assessment Approach Double Materiality Assessment Double Materiality Assessment Process Creation of Issue Pool To understand the economic, environmental and social impacts of JYP Entertainment's overall business activities, we analyzed global ESG disclosure guidelines, ESG evaluation indicators, and initiatives. This analysis resulted in a sustainability issue pool of 25 items. Selection of Material Issues Through the materiality assessment, we considered each issue's business significance and impact alongside environmental and social significance and impact, ultimately selecting five material issues that demonstrated high impact levels. The identified material issues and their respective risk and opportunity factors were reported to the ESG Committee, our highest decision-making body, and received approval for the material issue selection. Each material issue is incorporated into our enterprise-wide risk management strategy development, with annual implementation status monitoring.
Identification of IROs(Impact, Risk, Opportunity) For the 25 identified issues, we analyzed and derived issue-specific IROs. Based on domestic and international laws and regulations, ESG agendas, peer company cases, and industry trend-related news, we identified opportunity and risk factors from a business operation perspective that could occur in the short, medium, and long term, as well as positive and negative impact factors from environmental and social perspectives.
Double Materiality Assessment Considering the expertise and understanding of internal and external stakeholders, we categorized relevant stakeholder groups by issue and conducted online surveys and in-depth interviews. We evaluated the impact (magnitude and likelihood) of risks and opportunities from each issue on JYP Entertainment's business operations, while simultaneously assessing the positive and negative environmental and social impacts(scope, magnitude, and likelihood) of each issue.
Survey Overview Survey Method Online survey Survey Participants JYP Entertainment employees and external stakeholders(partners, analysts, academia, local communities, etc.) Focused Interview Overview Interview Participants Internal Artist Lable, HR Team, Information Security Team, IP Strategy Office, etc.
External Academia, analysts, journalists, government agencies, etc.

The FGIs proceeded in the following sequence: explanation of the materiality assessment's purpose and background, guidance on FGI operations, introduction of issue-specific trends and policies, Q&A with participants, and completion of importance and impact assessment templates for each issue. Through this process, we gained deeper insights into stakeholders' perceptions and expectations regarding JYP Entertainment's sustainability management issues, which will be utilized as resources for future strategy development.

r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP DEI JYP Entertainment is realizing the values of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion(DEI) both internally and externally to create content that resonates with fans worldwide. We continuously strive to secure diverse talent, provide equitable opportunities for everyone, and foster an inclusive organizational culture of mutual respect, ensuring that all members, including our employees and artists, can work in an environment where their unique backgrounds and talents are valued and respected. Through disclosing JYP Entertainment's DEI direction and current status, we wish to share our journey toward a more sustainable and healthy entertainment ecosystem.
2 3 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP DEI Definitions JYP Entertainment believes diversity, equity, and inclusion among all employees and artists involved in content creation to be essential for fostering meaningful communication through music in the global market and for delivering a positive influence to fans and other stakeholders. To support the sustainable growth of the company, employees, and artists—and to become a company loved by diverse fans worldwide—JYP has defined its own DEI concept.
JYP DEI Principles Based on our own definition of Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion(DEI) and our company philosophy and values, JYP Entertainment has established DEI principles to be observed throughout business operations and in working relationships among the company, employees, and artists.
JYP Entertainment actively leverages employees' diverse backgrounds and experiences, including gender, age, and nationality to make optimal decisions aligned with global music market trends, fan preferences, and evolving trends. We also respect artist diversity to enable fans of various genders, ages, and nationalities to engage more deeply with our content. JYP Entertainment guarantees equal opportunities for employees and artists to demonstrate their capabilities and talents without discrimination or bias, applying rational and objective indicators and standards across all HR processes. In addition, we operate communication channels where employees can freely discuss difficulties encountered in work performance or grievances arising from organizational life, due to differences in gender, age, nationality, and other factors. Furthermore, we are committed to creating an inclusive environment where diverse stakeholders, including fans, can interact with each other and conveniently access content.
Diversity Securing diversity among employees to create content loved by diverse fans in the global music market Equity Ensuring fairness in opportunities and processes so that employees can fully showcase their talents and be recognized for their contributions Inclusion Creating an inclusive organizational culture where employees feel a sense of belonging, engage deeply in their work, and respect one another JYP DEI Definitions JYP DEI Principles Leadership Diversity Equal Opportunity Inclusive Communication Employee Diversity Fair Evaluation & Compensation Fan Engagement Artists Diversity Healthy Workplace Culture Content Accessibility JYP Entertainment pursues diversity, independence, and expertise in its Board of Directors and management to ensure optimal decision-making for the company's long-term sustainable growth and development.
JYP Entertainment provides equal opportunities across all aspects of human resources management, including employee recruitment and training and does not discriminate based on individual background, identity, or cultural differences.
JYP Entertainment provides forums for employees from diverse backgrounds to freely communicate their thoughts and opinions to explore pathways for mutual growth between the company and its employees.
JYP Entertainment aims for an organizational culture where employees with diverse backgrounds and experiences can collaborate to support company growth and artist development.
JYP Entertainment evaluates employee performance using reasonable, objective procedures and standards, ensuring that compensation is based solely on fair and valid performance assessments without discrimination.
JYP Entertainment is expanding communication channels with fans, aiming to drive change for dreamers and push for a better society through content.
To grow as a leader in the global entertainment industry, JYP Entertainment discovers and develops artists with outstanding capabilities and talents regardless of background, including gender, nationality, and culture.
JYP Entertainment respects employees' individual identities and treats them equally, fostering a culture where they can demonstrate their capabilities and grow without bias or discrimination.
JYP Entertainment strives to create an environment where everyone can access content without restrictions related to physical ability, language, or other barriers.
Diversity Principles Equity Principles Inclusion Principles JYP DEI 2 4 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP DEI Management Framework JYP Entertainment is creating an organizational culture that respects DEI based on global principles and initiatives while strictly complying with domestic and international laws and regulations related to diversity, equity, and inclusion. To this end, we continuously survey and monitor external environments and internal conditions, and based on this analysis, we select DEI initiatives necessary for the growth and development of the company, employees, and artists. Particularly for areas requiring company-wide attention and sustained efforts, we establish medium- to long-term objectives and develop implementation plans to achieve these goals. During the implementation process, we periodically monitor progress and major achievement outcomes according to detailed activity plans. Any challenges encountered during execution are comprehensively reviewed to prepare alternatives, and initiatives are flexibly adjusted based on analysis of goal achievement feasibility. JYP DEI Implementation Areas JYP Entertainment operates recruitment practices considering diverse backgrounds to spread diversity values throughout the organization. Particularly, in order to support talent with various backgrounds and experiences in growing as artists, the company actively discovers artists regardless of gender and nationality through domestic and international auditions. In the equity area, JYP Entertainment focuses on preventing conflicts, discrimination, and harassment to create a fair and reasonable organizational culture. For this purpose, the company operates public reporting channels for sexual harassment and bullying and also runs counseling services for employees and artists. Specifically, the company reflects core values such as morality, integrity, and mutual respect in employee performance evaluations to foster a fair and reasonable organizational culture. In the inclusion area, the company operates various channels for open communication among employees, spreads inclusive content that global fans can empathize with, enhances content accessibility, and strengthens opinion gathering and communication activities with fans.

P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP Diversity JYP Entertainment values the diverse identities, backgrounds, experiences, and personalities of all members, including our board of directors, employees, and artists. Diversity is a foundation for sustainable growth in the evolving entertainment industry because when different people collaborate, they can create content with greater breadth and depth. JYP Entertainment aims to foster a culture where diverse members are respected and grow together while honoring each other's differences, thereby enabling us to deliver the finest music and performances to our fans globally. Board Diversity JYP Entertainment strives to compose a board of directors with diverse backgrounds so that the board can comprehensively consider the perspectives of various stakeholders, including shareholders, fans, employees, and partners, and make decisions for the company's long-term sustainable growth based on diverse and broad viewpoints. In the process of identifying and recommending director candidates, we assess independence and expertise as critical qualifications, and also conduct a thorough review of diversity considerations, including gender, age, nationality, professional experience, and personal background.
Employee Diversity In response to rising global demand for K-POP—evident in the growth of overseas concerts and MD sales—JYP Entertainment cultivates employees' global competencies and recruits individuals with diverse backgrounds, including different nationalities, to support global expansion. Employees who can understand and communicate country-specific cultural contexts and issues are able to identify potential risks when entering new markets and respond proactively to changes in key countries where we operate. To become a global leader in the entertainment industry, JYP Entertainment seeks to continuously provide education not only on capabilities needed for global markets and international business environments, but also on cultural sensitivity and other essential competencies for individual employees.

JYP DEI 2 8 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x JYP Equity JYP Entertainment seeks an environment where every member is respected and can grow equitably, without bias or discrimination. When everyone receives equal opportunities and can fully demonstrate their capabilities, organizations can develop in a more sustainable way. JYP Entertainment has established systems to ensure that all members, including employees and trainees, are fairly evaluated based on their performance and capabilities, supporting their continuous growth and motivation. Support for Language Learning and Certifications JYP Entertainment supports employees in pursuing necessary training and education autonomously across various fields, including languages and Microsoft Office Specialist(MOS) certification. This educational support is provided equally to all members regardless of their employment status. To create a more practical learning environment, we operate a hybrid approach combining online and offline training while also providing learning materials and tuition fees for certification programs.
Fair Evaluation and Compensation In the organizational and individual performance evaluation process, JYP Entertainment establishes reasonable and appropriate performance goals without discrimination based on diversity factors and evaluates goal achievement according to objective and reliable criteria. In addition, we ensure non-discrimination based on diversity through transparent compensation policies and payment procedures. Furthermore, we have established procedures to address any performance evaluation results or compensation that undermines diversity values. Team Leaders’ Leadership Workshop JYP Entertainment conducts leadership forum for team leaders, who serve as junior managers, to strengthen leadership capabilities and share organizational strategy and vision. To establish a culture where individuals demonstrate their capabilities without discrimination based on race, nationality, gender, age, disability, or other factors, we also provide evaluator training to ensure fairness and consistency in assessments. JYP Entertainment will continue to operate a trusted HR system through enhanced leadership capabilities and fair performance evaluations.
Fair Auditions To discover creative and proactive talent, JYP Entertainment conducts auditions for applicants with diverse talents, regardless of gender, nationality, or cultural background. Beyond open auditions, we operate a global audition system with overseas on-site visits and the JYP Online Audition System, enabling easy participation through online channels anytime, anywhere. By operating audition programs that discover potential talent across the culturally diverse global music markets worldwide, we strive to develop the next generation of global artists.

Through an inclusive organizational culture that respects diverse experiences and perspectives, we support members to collaborate with high levels of trust. Gen Z Employee Roundtable JYP Entertainment has conducted Gen Z employee forums directly hosted by the CEO to broaden intergenerational understanding and facilitate smooth communication. These forums offer an opportunity for Gen Z employees, who are closest to consumer demographics, share the values and consumption trends they consider important while discussing JYP Entertainment's business direction. Through the CEO's direct engagement with employee perspectives and authentic communication, we are building a trust-based organizational culture.
Town Hall Meeting with New Hires JYP Entertainment regularly conducts new employee meetings hosted by J.Y. These sessions provide Q&A opportunities covering overall company life, entertainment industry and organizational direction, health management, and other topics, thereby creating an environment for open discussion without bias. Through these initiatives, we support further strengthening organizational identity and inspiring members' growth motivation.
Inclusive Workplace Culture JYP Entertainment fosters an inclusive culture where diverse members can connect and communicate without discrimination through employee club activities. These clubs operate through voluntary participation by members, fostering organic interactions based on shared interests and preferences. They contribute to respecting and embracing diversity within the organization. Currently operating clubs include sports clubs such as running, soccer, and basketball; cultural arts clubs that enjoy dance and cultural activities; and hobby clubs such as board games and tea ceremonies. All clubs operate openly, allowing anyone to freely participate. Through these activities, we expand understanding and communication among members while realizing a healthy organizational culture based on respect and consideration.

JYP Entertainment has established a governance framework centered around the ESG Committee to develop and implement company-wide environmental management strategies. We became the first company in Korea's entertainment industry to voluntarily participate in 'K-RE100' and manage climate change risks and opportunities based on our 2050 carbon neutrality roadmap. 3 3 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Environmental Management Organizational Chart · CSR Team · Management Support Team · IP Strategy Office · BLUE GARAGE · F&B Team · CSR Team Governance Board of Directors The ESG Committee under the Board of Directors establishes and adopts environmental management objectives and deliberates on important matters such as building environmental management foundations. Additionally, it conducts annual reviews of company-wide environmental management performance and status, including greenhouse gas emissions and biodiversity protection, and it also reviews and approves essential agendas for business strategy implementation and management activities. Top Management The CEO periodically reviews company-wide environmental management implementation plans, execution status, and improvement outcomes while discussing response strategies for major environmental risks. Additionally, the CEO oversees requirements for expanding and internalizing environmental management, and he brings issues that may significantly impact business strategy to the Board of Directors for discussion.
ESG Council The ESG Council shares environmental issues in alignment with environmental management strategic direction and discusses corresponding response measures. During this process, matters expected to have significant medium- to long-term impact on JYP Entertainment are reviewed and presented as Board agenda items. Through this approach, environmental management strategies are continuously monitored and improved to ensure practical implementation.
ESG Working Group We develop and systematically implement environmental management strategic initiatives centered on the Environmental Unit within the ESG Working Group. Based on our environmental management policy, we specify concrete objectives for implementing environmental management and identify departmental improvement areas. We continuously monitor the overall implementation process to enhance practical outcomes while strengthening internal management systems for smooth execution of strategic initiatives.
Dedicated Department JYP Entertainment fosters organic collaboration among departments to practice environmental management. We implement environmental management policies and execute environmental management strategies centered on the ESG Working Group Environmental Unit. CSR Team and Management Support Team are dedicated to 'Climate Action', managing energy and greenhouse gas emissions, and IP Strategy Office, F&B Team, BLUE GARAGE, and CSR Team are responsible for 'Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value', creating an eco-friendly culture within the company and conducting environmentally friendly business activities.
Environmental Management Climate Action Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value CEO ESG Council ESG Working Group Environmental Unit Board of Directors ESG Committee 3 4 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Strategy Environmental Management Policy JYP Entertainment has established environmental management policies with CEO approval and officially declared it to proactively address the climate crisis and achieve sustainable environmental management. Through this, we aim to minimize environmental impacts from our business activities. Our environmental management policy consists of key focus areas for implementing environmental management: climate action, biodiversity protection and resource circulation, eco-friendly value expansion, and environmental regulatory compliance. Based on our environmental management policy, JYP Entertainment manages company-wide environmental performance to gradually improve environmental impacts from business activities and promote eco-friendly values alongside all stakeholders, including employees, artists, and fans.
Environmental Awareness Integration Environmental Education Implementation JYP Entertainment operates environmental education programs tailored to each department's characteristics and responsibilities, supporting all employees in internalizing and practicing eco-friendly values. We provide basic training for new employees to encourage interest and action on environmental issues, and we also guide Artist Label on methods to reduce environmental impacts during content production processes. Additionally, we share domestic and international sustainable performance practices with our concert business division and promote energy conservation in stages and venues. Furthermore, we collaborate with merchandise planning and development teams to discuss eco-friendly merchandise development strategies, ensuring environmental considerations are integrated from the product planning stage.
Eco-friendly Office Development JYP Entertainment has implemented an electronic payment system to establish a paperless work environment, reducing paper consumption while enhancing operational efficiency. To minimize environmental impacts within office spaces, we use GREEN GUARD certified eco-friendly office furniture, creating a healthy and sustainable work environment. Moreover, we practice eco-friendly principles in our internal food culture. Our company cafeteria 'JYPBOB' and in-house café 'JYP Soulcup' center their menus around organic, pesticide-free, and antibiotic-free ingredients. To reduce environmental pollution, we minimize single-use items and actively utilize reusable tableware. Eco-friendly Shopping Bags JYP Entertainment has converted shopping bags used to deliver goods to stakeholders such as internal members, external partners, and customers into eco-friendly materials. Made of 100% sugar cane, the bag is made of wood-free Earl's Pack and does not use adhesive or coating to reduce the overall environmental impact. Since it does not contain petrochemical components or utilize GMO plants, the ingredients are harmless to the human body. The bags are designed considering the user's health as well as the environment when using these shopping bags.

We comprehensively review environmental risks that may arise throughout business activities and organizational operations, including album production, performance planning, merchandise manufacturing, and content distribution. By analyzing relevant domestic and international regulatory changes, industry trends, and stakeholder requirements, we identify potential risk factors and improvement opportunities.
Identified risks are evaluated through both quantitative and qualitative approaches based on likelihood of occurrence and financial/non-financial impact levels, prioritizing specific risk and opportunity factors in key areas such as climate action, energy management, and waste reduction. Subsequently, we develop implementation plans by specifying risk response levels and approaches based on priorities. During this process, we prepare execution roadmaps linked to ESG strategies, considering not only short-term measures but also potential risk transformation from medium- to long-term perspectives.
In the execution phase, each department takes ownership of periodically monitoring implementation task progress, with flexible frameworks enabling swift adjustment of action plans when circumstances change or risk reassessment becomes necessary. Implementation status for each phase is regularly reported to the ESG Committee and senior management, ensuring transparent management under enterprise-wide accountability.
Environmental Risk Identification and Response Framework ·  Identify environmental risks and opportunities that may arise during business and organizational operations ·  Analyze domestic and international environmental regulations and market changes to identify potential risks and improvement opportunities ·  Conduct quantitative and qualitative assessments of risk likelihood and financial/non-financial impact levels ·  Prioritize risks and opportunity factors by category, including climate change, resource utilization, and waste generation ·  Determine risk response levels and approaches based on priorities ·  Develop and implement action plans tailored to specific issues ·  Monitor and track implementation of action initiatives ·  Provide regular reporting to ESG Committee and senior management Environmental Management Identify risks and opportunities Create and execute a response plan Risk and opportunity analysis Monitoring and reporting results 3 6 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Governance Board of Directors As the highest decision-making body, the Board of Directors deliberates and decides on major ESG-related matters, including climate change, and also oversees management execution. It independently and objectively evaluates key climate change-related issues and actively incorporates them into major decisions that significantly impact business strategy. The Board also reviews climate action policies, including greenhouse gas reduction targets and implementation strategies.
Dedicated department JYP Entertainment sets carbon reduction targets and develops implementation measures for greenhouse gas reduction and energy management through CSR Team and Management Support Team within the ESG Working Group Environmental Unit to establish company-wide medium- to long-term carbon emission reduction strategies. We also continuously collaborate with relevant departments to establish climate change transition/physical risk responses and perform climate action activities including Scope 3 inventory expansion.
Climate change performance management JYP Entertainment has introduced environment related KPIs, including climate action, within our ESG KPI framework. The CEO and management are evaluated based on climate action and environmental management KPIs. Key performance indicators include greenhouse gas emissions management, renewable energy transition, and carbon neutrality implementation. Top Management The Chief Executive Officer(CEO), serving as the company-wide environmental executive, reviews and oversees major environmental matters including carbon neutrality promotion strategies, greenhouse gas reduction measures, energy efficiency improvements, and climate actions. Climate action matters expected to substantially impact medium- to long-term business prospects are presented as Board agenda items. Ultimately, through deliberation and resolution by the Board as the highest decision making body, we disclose JYP Entertainment’s key management policies to stakeholders.
Climate Action Organizational Chart CEO ESG Council ESG Working Group Environment Unit · CSR Team · Management Support Team Climate Action Board of Directors ESG Committee Climate Action 3 7 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Strategy 2050 Carbon Neutrality Plan JYP Entertainment is implementing a multifaceted greenhouse gas reduction strategy to achieve carbon neutrality by 2050. We are working toward carbon neutrality by updating medium- to long-term objectives and implementation plans, considering domestic and international climate change laws and regulations, market trends, stakeholder requirements, and alignment with our business strategies.
Moving forward, we will gradually expand the greenhouse gas inventory management scope to overall Scope 3 and strengthen greenhouse gas reduction efforts across the entire value chain to continuously pursue Net Zero achievement by 2050.
GHG Reduction Plan Renewable Energy Transition Expansion JYP Entertainment became the first company in Korea's entertainment industry to voluntarily participate in the Korean RE100(K-RE100) campaign, aiming to source 100% of our business electricity needs from renewable energy. In 2024, we replaced all electricity consumption(2,149.09MWh) at our headquarters and subsidiary BLUE GARAGE with renewable energy, utilizing renewable sources for both office operations and music recording processes. To gradually expand our RE100 initiative, we are evaluating various approaches including Renewable Energy Certificate(REC) purchases, self-generation facilities(solar power), and Power Purchase Agreements(PPAs).
High-Efficiency Equipment Introduction We continuously monitor energy consumption across domestic and international business locations, enhancing energy management through practical measures such as facility operation optimization, high-efficiency equipment introduction, and usage pattern improvements. Particularly to reduce emissions from stationary combustion equipment(boilers, heaters, etc.), we are considering high-efficiency control and monitoring system implementation from the design phase of our new headquarters, targeting eco-friendly building certification. We aim to create sustainable spaces by maximizing energy efficiency through various eco-friendly infrastructure including high-efficiency lighting, electric vehicle charging infrastructure, and renewable energy facilities.
Low-Carbon Vehicle Conversion To reduce greenhouse gas emissions from mobile combustion, we plan to systematically convert existing gasoline and diesel vehicles to hybrid and electric alternatives. Our medium- to long-term objectives include converting 50% of our operational fleet to electric vehicles by 2032 and achieving 100% conversion by 2035, thereby realizing carbon emission reductions within Scope 1.

We have established clear criteria for defining material climate change impacts and discuss them through the ESG Council and Environmental Unit within the ESG Working Group.
Climate-related risks are categorized into four levels: Attention required, Moderate, Important, and Severe. These are based on comprehensive assessment of occurrence likelihood, impact severity, and resilience. Each risk is assigned a grade according to evaluation results. Risks with important or severe-level impacts are considered material business risks, particularly from a financial perspective when revenue decreases of 1% or more are expected, which we classify as financially material risks.
Risk Management and Monitoring JYP Entertainment has established and operates its own processes to identify and proactively address climate change risks. For businesses with potential climate issue exposure, the ESG Committee conducts advance reviews of comprehensive ESG-perspective risks including strategic, operational, and financial risks, as well as environmental pollution, ethics, policy violations, and stakeholder interest infringement.
Additionally, JYP Entertainment selects key management items reflecting risk likelihood and impact from short-, medium-, and long-term perspectives, performs financial impact analysis, and incorporates findings into enterprise-wide strategies and business implementation.
Establishment of Enterprise-wide Risk Integration System JYP Entertainment integrates climate change-related risks and opportunities into our enterprise-wide risk management framework for comprehensive management and operations, regularly monitoring potential risk impacts on the company.
We comprehensively analyze and respond to various risks that may arise throughout business processes, including environmental, safety, and domestic/international regulatory risks alongside climate change.
The CEO establishes and regularly reviews enterprise-wide risk response strategies, including climate change, closely analyzing business impacts of such risks while performing management and oversight roles. Material climate change-related issues and risks are reported to the ESG Committee for proactive response and management of potential future risk factors.
ESG Committee ESG Council ESG Working Group ESG Committee Climate Action Climate change Risk identification, Assessment, and Management Process Identify climate change issues Identify risk/opportunity factors and assess impact Examine priorities and develop response strategies Monitoring and assessment Create and execute a response plan Integrated enterprise risk management STEP 01 STEP 03 STEP 05 STEP 02 STEP 04 STEP 06 3 9 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Risk Management Climate Risk and Opportunity Analysis In accordance with TCFD recommendations, JYP Entertainment identified seven climate change-related risks and opportunities based on our risk management process. Risk factors were subdivided into physical risks and transition risks, while opportunity factors were analyzed focusing on climate change impacts from product and service perspectives.
Additionally, identified climate change risks and opportunities were categorized by occurrence timeframe(short-, medium-, and long-term) and impact scope, comprehensive financial impact reviews were conducted to analyze climate change effects on overall business operations. Based on these results, we established phased greenhouse gas reduction targets and medium- to long-term reduction frameworks, developing detailed reduction plans for each business activity.
Strategies for Addressing Climate Change Risks JYP Entertainment recognizes climate action as a critical management priority and implements various response activities. We monitor and manage annual energy consumption across domestic and international business locations while actively pursuing greenhouse gas reduction through renewable energy procurement.
JYP Entertainment became the first company in Korea's entertainment industry to voluntarily participate in the Korean RE100(K-RE100) campaign in 2021, achieving 100% renewable energy conversion for headquarters electricity consumption(2,047.945MWh). In 2024, our subsidiary BLUE GARAGE joined the RE100 initiative, replacing 100% of business activity electricity requirements(101.115MWh) with renewable energy-generated power.
We are introducing and operating environmentally conscious technologies, including digital-based albums, which enable music and content access via QR codes. To reduce the environmental impact of product packaging, we are gradually transitioning to eco-friendly materials for packaging for products sold through our merchandise platform 'FANS SHOP'. Currently, we utilize FSC-certified boxes and bagasse paper bags(environmentally friendly packaging material made from 100% sugarcane bagasse, a byproduct of sugar extraction, without paper coating). We plan to continue implementing improvements to minimize environmental impact.

Based on measured data, projected emissions are thoroughly analyzed and regularly reported to the ESG Council to enhance transparency and reliability of emission management. Additionally, we undergo rigorous third-party verification by independent organizations to confirm systematic establishment of GHG inventories by emission source at each location and ensure reliability of compiled data. This enhances quantitative data credibility and continuously improves the effectiveness of our emission reduction strategies.
Scope 1 and 2 GHG Reduction Activities and Energy Management JYP Entertainment has established greenhouse gas reduction targets aligned with the 2050 Carbon Neutrality Roadmap, implementing various activities consistent with carbon neutrality strategies through RE100 implementation, renewable energy expansion, energy efficiency improvements, and other initiatives. Particularly for energy conservation in office spaces, practice rooms, and performance venues, we minimize energy consumption through optimal heating and cooling operations, low-power LED lighting, and automatic monitor sleep mode systems. We also foster organization-wide eco-friendly culture by continuously identifying and implementing energy-saving initiatives enabling voluntary participation by employees.
JYP Entertainment will continue developing practical implementation measures to achieve carbon neutrality objectives while building efficient energy management systems and transitioning to clean energy.
Scope 3 GHG Emissions Management Scope 3 Emissions Calculation In accordance with GHG Protocol Scope 3 calculation standards, JYP Entertainment has calculated Scope 3 emissions utilizing internal data and external indicators appropriate for each category. Moving forward, we will expand data management scope and develop additional inventories to enhance calculation methodologies and transparently disclose more reliable information.
Scope 3 Emissions Verification JYP Entertainment undergoes annual third-party verification of Scope 3 emission calculation procedures and results. Verification is conducted according to international greenhouse gas verification standards(ISO 14064-1, ISO 14064-3) and confirms compliance with international standards(GHG Protocol) used in developing calculation methodologies.

Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value Promoting Eco-Friendly Values Organizational Chart CEO ESG Council ESG Working Group Environment Unit · IP Strategy Office · BLUE GARAGE · F&B Team · CSR Team Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value Board of Directors ESG Committee 4 2 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Strategy Sustainable Business Eco-Friendly Albums JYP Entertainment introduces and operates environmentally conscious technologies by producing digital-based albums that provide music and content access through QR codes. Through this approach, we aim to reduce resource waste and promote sustainable music consumption culture.
In 2024, we released 17 albums in digital format, including TWICE's "Strategy" and Stray Kids' "SKZHOP HIPTAPE '合(HOP)'," with plans to gradually expand this practice to future album releases. Additionally, we actively incorporate eco-friendly materials in producing both digital and physical album components, utilizing FSC-certified paper, soy-based ink, cornstarch PLA materials, bagasse paper, and bio-Ecogen plastic materials. We minimize hazardous waste emissions through printing technology using KODAK processless plates.
Release a digital album JYP Entertainment has designed digital-based album production to reduce physical resource consumption while enabling users to conveniently enjoy music and artist content. QR scanning provides access to music playback as well as diverse supplementary content including digital photo cards and message videos, creating a new format that is both environmentally conscious and collectible.
In 2024, we produced digital versions for all album releases by DAY6, TWICE, Stray Kids, ITZY, Xdinary Heroes, and others. We also work to reduce environmental impact during digital album releases by utilizing PLA packaging bags and configuring kits with bio-Ecogen cases.
Making music with eco-friendly materials JYP Entertainment actively explores eco-friendly material usage in album production. For Stray Kids' 2024 album <SKZHOP HIPTAPE ‘合(HOP)’>, we incorporated eco-friendly pulp paper made from sugarcane bagasse for album components. This material, produced from byproducts remaining after sugarcane extraction instead of traditional wood pulp, can contribute to resource circulation and carbon emission reduction. NEXZ’s <Ride the Vibe> and TWICE’s <Strategy> utilized environmentally friendly corrugated cardboard produced from recycled materials for album packaging. JYP Entertainment is committed to substantially improving resource utilization methods and production processes in album manufacturing beyond simple packaging material changes.

In the merchandise planning and production process, we establish procedures to assess the environmental impact of materials in advance and examine feasibility of applying eco-friendly alternatives.
We prioritize officially certified sustainable materials for paper goods and apparel products while monitoring production processes. For other merchandise including keychains, tumblers, and reusable bags, we gradually explore eco-friendly material transition possibilities based on product viability reviews and fan feedback.
Eco-friendly Packaging Transition JYP Entertainment recognizes merchandise packaging's environmental impact and continuously pursues transitioning FANS SHOP packaging to environmentally friendly materials. Following gradual eco-friendly packaging material introduction since 2022, we currently utilize Forest Stewardship Council(FSC) certified boxes and paper bags made from sugarcane bagasse to minimize environmental impact.
This represents a shift toward reducing unnecessary plastic usage and actively introducing easily recyclable materials. We will continue expanding eco-friendly packaging material usage and will continuously improve product packaging approaches to reduce environmental impact.
Sustainable Material Integration JYP Entertainment gradually expands sustainable material utilization throughout merchandise planning and production. To minimize environmental impact during production processes while maintaining product quality and completeness, we prioritize the use of certified eco-friendly materials such as FSC-certified paper and soy-based ink while exploring alternative material applicability to reduce plastic usage.
Particularly for apparel merchandise, we proactively assess eco-friendly fabric usage considering fabric characteristics and production conditions, accumulating practical implementation cases.

Rather than disposing of the banners used in the tournament after single use, we collaborated with social enterprise 'Touch4Good' to upcycle the JYPBT banners into practical sneaker bags. The bags were made with banners and upcycled waterproof fabric with added zipper pockets to enhance practicality. Additionally, by upcycling a total of 96.6kg of tournament banners, we reduced unnecessary waste discharge and contributed to greenhouse gas emission reduction. The upcycling sneaker bags will be used as commemorative items for the JYPBT tournament.
Promotion of Eco-Friendly Value Upcycling EDM Volunteer Group Uniforms JYP Entertainment created group uniforms for employees participating in EDM volunteer activities. To minimize waste discharge and embody the value of resource circulation, the company newly created denim aprons for volunteer group uniforms using fabric from discarded jeans. The sturdy denim fabric and utilization of pockets and buttons enhanced convenience of use, and EDM badges were created so that members could decorate their aprons as desired. We utilized group uniforms that can be worn comfortably during volunteer activities, enabling participating members to feel a sense of belonging, reflect on the meaning of environmental protection, and participate in volunteer activities with joy.
1% for the Planet Since 2020, JYP Entertainment has been an official member of '1% for the Planet', a global environmental network with companies, non-profit organizations, and individuals worldwide as part of global solidarity efforts to create healthy planetary environments. Through this membership, we maintain ongoing collaboration with domestic and international environmental organizations, providing diverse support and practical contributions to address pressing environmental challenges. Recognizing marine ecosystem protection importance, we donate 1% of annual sales from our in-house café 'JYP Soulcup' to OSEAN to actively participate in marine plastic problem resolution and marine debris reduction activities. Moving forward, JYP Entertainment will continue to seek sustainable practices and change for environmental protection through professional organization partnerships.

Based on alignment with sustainable business strategies, we examine each initiative's feasibility from multiple perspectives, centering on JYP's eco-friendly values and objectives. Additionally, we reflect the reactions and expectation levels of key stakeholders, including fans, to secure acceptance and consensus while comparing and analyzing the implementation levels of the peer industry to closely understand development trends and practical applicability of eco-friendly materials and technologies. Additionally, we actively promote resource circulation activities in organizational operations, including a reusable cup rental system introduction and upcycled item production, achieving reduction in waste generation and enhancement of resource efficiency.
From an economic perspective, we minimize implementation burdens through comprehensive analysis of cost structures, drivers of unit price increases, and long-term profitability. We continuously monitor policy trends to enable proactive responses to changes in environmental regulations and institutional frameworks.

In 2024, under the theme 'Action for Zero', artists and fans together practiced the 3R movement(Reduce, Reuse, Recycle) to reduce waste flowing into the ocean. Through 2,480 participations over 4 weeks, a total of KRW 49.6 million in donations was raised, which was donated to OSEAN(East Asia Sea Community Ocean), a non-profit organization focused on solving marine waste issues, to conduct coastal cleanup activities. Activities were conducted at 30 marine waste concentration areas nationwide with the participation of 25 organizations and 536 people, and the total cumulative length of cleaned coastlines reached 10.4km.
Through cleanup activities, approximately 12.8 tons(99,190 pieces) of marine waste were collected. The main items collected were styrofoam buoys, PET bottles, and plastic bags in that order. In particular, 11,320 buoys, which are major pollutants of marine ecosystems, were collected, resulting in the effect of preventing the generation of approximately 4.4 billion microplastics in advance.
Coast Stabilization Activities (Korea Federation for Environmental Movement in Masan·Changwon·Jinhae) Additionally, JYP Entertainment collaborated with the East Asian Seas Action Network(OSEAN) and ‘Ocean Conservancy’, the international coastal cleanup headquarters, to operate International Coastal Cleanup(ICC) seminars and exhibition booths during the '5th Intergovernmental Negotiating Committee on Plastic Pollution(INC-5)', raising awareness of the importance of coastal cleanup activities to the international community. Moving forward, JYP Entertainment plans to take the lead in creating a better Earth through the 'LOVE EARTH CHALLENGE' campaign, where artists and fans together spread eco-friendly values and enable future generations to nurture their dreams in healthy soil.

This system allows employees to easily borrow reusable cups as an alternative to disposable cups, even when employees haven't prepared personal tumblers. We utilize RFID technology to track and manage quantities collected. To enhance usability, we established a reusable cup rental space at our in-house café JYP Soulcup and positioned recycling bin at major building locations, enabling everyone to conveniently utilize reusable cups. Additionally, we encourage usage by providing beverage discounts for reusable cup use and carbon neutrality points while displaying greenhouse gas reduction amounts on screen displays to heighten employee environmental awareness. Through the introduction of reusable cup rental system in 2024, we improved average monthly reusable cup usage rates to 63%, which is a 40% increase compared to the same period last year. Throughout the year, we reduced disposable cup consumption by utilizing 41,770 reusable containers, including tumblers and reusable cups. This achieved environmental benefits of approximately 1,469kgCO2eq greenhouse gas emission reductions.

4 9 J Y P P r o f i l e W a y t o S u s t a i n a b l e E n t e r t a i n m e n t E S G P e r f o r m a n c e A p p e n d i x Human Rights Management Governance Board of Directors The ESG Committee serves as the highest decision-making body overseeing human rights management, establishing policies and directions while conducting advisory and deliberation functions. Additionally, it makes decisions on major human rights related matters and reviews implementation performance to promote continuous improvement and development. Top Management CEO serves as the executive responsible for human rights management, listening to human rights related issues and opinions while making key decisions. They establish frameworks to prevent and manage human rights risks throughout business operations while ensuring effective implementation. Management also regularly monitors human rights related issues and reports significant matters to the Board of Directors.
Human Rights Management Council The Human Rights Management Council functions as the core organization responsible for implementing human rights management. It conducts human rights impact assessments to identify human rights risks for each stakeholder group and develop improvement measures. The Council also conducts diverse human rights education and campaigns for all members — including employees, artists, trainees, and creators to foster a culture of human rights protection. Furthermore, we implement prompt measures to ensure remedial actions are provided if human rights violations occur, thereby establishing a practical human rights protection framework.

Based on this commitment, we have established a human rights management policy with CEO approval to further strengthen our human rights management. This policy applies to all stakeholders related to JYP Entertainment, including all members of JYP Entertainment's headquarters, subsidiaries, and overseas affiliate, as well as business partners, users, and local communities with collaborative relationships. It encompasses key elements for protecting human rights, including including respect for diversity and prohibition of discrimination, compliance with working conditions and hours, dignified treatment, freedom of association and collective bargaining, prohibition of forced labor, and worker health and safety, demonstrating JYP Entertainment's strong commitment to implementation.
Human Rights Issue Management JYP Entertainment manages enterprise-wide human rights issues in an integrated manner based on the human rights management process outlined in our Human Rights Management Policy. First, we establish and declare a human rights management policy articulating our clear commitment to human rights respect, building and operating a human rights management system for implementation. Subsequently, we periodically monitor and assess human rights risks inherent throughout business operations, establishing effective improvement measures for identified risks followed by systematic implementation. Furthermore, even after improvement activities, we continue issue monitoring and management to ensure that human rights management does not remain simply a declaration but leads to implementation and immediate improvement measures. Through this approach, we will continue to realize substantial, and responsible change, and responses beyond mere compliance.
Human Rights Awareness JYP Entertainment conducts human rights education for all members to foster a culture of respect for human rights and proactively prevent human rights risks, aiming to create healthy work environments where all employees can collaborate with mutual respect. Through these approaches, we aim to create healthy work environments where all members can respectfully collaborate. We conduct workplace harassment and sexual harassment prevention education and disability awareness improvement education for all employees annually. Additionally, we provide case-based education on industry-related human rights issues and status for managers, supporting them to practice human rights protection and respect in their work. Moving forward, we will expand the scope of education to include not only employees but also artists, writers, and trainees, spreading an inclusive organizational culture company-wide.

In the short term, we focus on establishing human rights management systems by enacting human rights management policies and conducting human rights impact assessments to identify key risks and derive improvement tasks. We have also established Human Rights Grievance Handling Channels to allow employees to report human rights grievances and enhanced human rights sensitivity through education for members and managers. In the medium term, we aim to internalize human rights management by refining dedicated organizational systems and plan to regularly conduct impact assessments to implement improvement tasks. We will enhance effectiveness through diversifying Human Rights Grievance Handling Channels, subdividing education topics, and conducting member satisfaction surveys. In the long term, we plan to expand the scope of human rights impact assessments to all business sites for advanced human rights management and improve management policies based on due diligence. Through this phased approach, we will establish human rights management as a core element of corporate operations and build a global-level human rights management model.
Diversity Goals JYP Entertainment has designated workforce diversity and inclusion expansion as a key priority, establishing medium to long-term diversity promotion objectives for implementation. Considering the characteristics of the entertainment industry, we aim to consistently maintain female employee and female manager ratios at 50% or higher respectively. To achieve these targets, we will continuously explore specific implementation initiatives to strengthen diversity and inclusion capabilities.
